title: LME Aluminium Price Surge Hits $3,269/Tonne in March 2026
summary: LME aluminium prices reached $3,269 per tonne in March 2026 due to inventory drawdowns and supply constraints. The surge reflects tightening market fundamentals amid ongoing global supply challenges.

title: U.S. aluminum prices hit record highs amid tariffs, low supply
summary: U.S. aluminum prices surged 40% to over $5,200 per metric ton following 50% tariffs on imports, with Midwest premiums hitting a record 96 cents per pound. Global LME prices rose over 20% in the past year due to supply shortages, exacerbating the tariff impacts and threatening manufacturers.

title: Aluminum prices on March 12, 2026, approached a four-year high
summary: On March 12, 2026, LME three-month aluminum prices rose 1.32% to $3,502.50 per ton, nearing a four-year high. This increase highlights continued upward pressure on aluminum markets.

title: After US tariffs, aluminium price surge due to Middle East conflict causing concern to Coca-Cola
summary: Aluminium prices surged to a four-year high near $3,500 per tonne in March 2026, driven by US tariffs and Middle East conflicts. The price escalation is raising concerns for major consumers like Coca-Cola amid supply disruptions.

title: Aluminum prices surge to a four-year high amid war
summary: Aluminum prices hit a near four-year high of around $3,540 per ton due to Middle East tensions potentially disrupting supply from key producing regions. The video discusses impacts on global metal markets from ongoing conflicts.

title: Aluminum prices in March 2026
summary: Aluminum prices on LME surged over 22% year-to-date in 2026, outpacing gold's 15% rise, amid Middle East geopolitical tensions affecting the Strait of Hormuz—a key route for 20% of global aluminum supply outside China.

title: Aluminium reaches four-year highs due to Middle East shipping disruptions
summary: Aluminum prices hit four-year highs amid fears of prolonged Middle East shipping disruptions affecting supply. China's primary aluminum production rose 3% in January-February 2026 to 7.53 million metric tonnes, driven by improved profit margins of 7,879 Yuan per ton. This increase reflects higher profitability despite broader industrial overcapacity concerns.

title: Alcoa draws new aluminum orders as Gulf states curb production
summary: Alcoa, the largest US aluminum producer, reports increased orders and inquiries for Q2 and H2 as Middle East smelters curtail production due to Strait of Hormuz closure. Gulf states, producing 9% of global supply, cut output to conserve raw materials, redirecting Alcoa's alumina shipments elsewhere, likely to China. Aluminum prices soared to 2022 highs last week before some gains pared.

title: Aluminium-Free High-Barrier Films Market to Reach USD 3.82 Billion by 2035 as Sustainable Packaging Replaces Foil-Based Structures
summary: The global aluminum-free high-barrier films market, valued at USD 1.65 billion in 2025, is projected to reach USD 3.82 billion by 2035, driven by innovations replacing aluminum foil with recyclable mono-materials, bio-based polymers, and advanced coatings. Companies like Mondi Group, Toppan, Constantia Flexibles, and Jindal Poly Films are investing heavily in production expansions for sustainable packaging alternatives that maintain high barrier performance while reducing environmental impact.

title: Potential impact on Qatalum aluminium production
summary: Hydro reports a potential impact on Qatalum's aluminum production, following strong 2025 performance with NOK 1.3 billion adjusted net income on a 50% basis. The update highlights ongoing operational considerations in sustainable aluminum production amid global industry challenges.

title: GHG Emissions Standards; Aluminum Prices Surge
summary: S&P Global's daily update covers surging aluminum prices alongside GHG emissions standards and US labor market insights. The report notes market dynamics influencing aluminum technology and sustainability efforts in the industry.

title: US flat steel pricing continues climb, lower March scrap could be balanced by low steel imports
summary: Weekly US flat steel prices continued their recent ascent, with hot-rolled coils finishing the week up another $10/nt. Scrap prices, which rose $30/gt, could finish near or less than $395-405/gt. The article notes that lower March scrap levels could be balanced by low steel imports.

title: Steel Consuming Market Outlook March 2026
summary: Construction planning is cooling in early 2026 but remains at historically high levels. Residential housing construction is rebounding with January 2026 housing starts up 7.2% from December, driven by steel-intensive multi-family projects, though HVAC equipment shipments remain under pressure.

title: Market Update: March 2026
summary: Domestic pricing continues to climb above $1,000 per ton, driven by continued trade restrictions limiting access to North American supply outside the United States and ongoing market pressures.

title: Tata Steel Teams With University of Science and Technology Beijing for Green Steel Research
summary: Tata Steel has signed a memorandum of understanding with the University of Science and Technology Beijing to collaborate on advancing green steelmaking technologies. This partnership aims to drive innovation in sustainable steel production. It focuses on reducing emissions through research into low-carbon methods.

title: Primetals Technologies Delivers Fourth Continuous Caster to HBIS Tangsteel
summary: HBIS Tangsteel has started up a new slab caster, its fourth from Primetals Technologies, at the Tangshan Laoting plant in China. This technological advancement enhances steel production efficiency. It supports modern manufacturing processes with potential sustainability benefits through improved operations.

title: Viewpoint: US copper faces uncertain 2026 outlook
summary: Goldman Sachs forecasts copper prices to average $5.17/lb in 2026, up from $4.82/lb in 2025, amid supply concerns and potential US tariffs. The copper market saw a narrowing surplus in 2025, with other banks like Citigroup predicting prices above $5.90/lb by Q2 2026. China copper prices fell due to higher stocks and a stronger dollar, with SHFE inventories rising significantly.

title: Copper Prices Are Forecast to Decline Somewhat from Record Highs in 2026
summary: Goldman Sachs expects copper prices to decline to $10,000-$11,000 per tonne in 2026 due to a global surplus, despite record highs earlier. Chinese demand weakened in Q4 2025, but long-term demand from power infrastructure and potential US tariffs could support prices beyond 2026. The market is projected to end 2025 in a 500kt surplus, narrowing to 160kt in 2026.

title: Copper Prices Surge to Record Highs
summary: Comex copper futures hit $6.58 per pound on January 29, 2026, near 1972 highs, with banks diverging on outlooks: Goldman Sachs sees LME at $13,000/ton in Q1 easing to $11,000 by year-end. J.P. Morgan forecasts peaks near $5.67/lb with deficits, while risks include rising inventories and trade conflicts. US tariff decisions expected by mid-2026 could boost stockpiling.

title: Don't Regret This: Bullish Copper Outlook for 2026 and Beyond
summary: Panelists Rick Rule, Ivan Bebek, and Lobo Tiggre discuss copper's all-time highs and bullish 2026 outlook due to supply deficits, growing demand from electrification and AI, and a $250 billion capital gap for new mines. Challenges include no major discoveries, permitting delays, and government interventions. They emphasize arithmetic of tightening supply amid rising demand.

title: Copper Marches Toward Record Highs
summary: Copper futures rose above $5.8 per pound, supported by tightening supply from US tariff concerns redirecting shipments and a strike at Chile’s Mantoverde mine. Prices fell below $5.5/lb earlier amid rising inventories but are moving toward records. Last year’s 40% gain was driven by supply shortfalls and demand.

title: 3-Month Nickel Commodity Price Rises Toward US$17,420/Ton Level (Tuesday, March 17, 2026)
summary: The 3-month forward nickel commodity price rose 0.49% to US$17,420 per ton on March 17, 2026, marking the highest jump that week. Year-to-date growth stands at 2.99%, with weekly average at US$17,480 per ton. Over the past five years, annual averages have trended downward, but monthly prices over the last 12 months have risen.

title: [SMM Nickel Midday Review] On March 19, nickel prices plunged sharply, and the US Fed kept interest rates unchanged at 3.5%-3.75%
summary: Nickel prices plunged sharply on March 19 amid the US Federal Reserve's decision to keep interest rates unchanged at 3.5%-3.75%. This midday review highlights the significant drop in nickel prices. Market confidence was impacted, as noted in related NPI reviews.

title: [NPI Daily Review] Sharp Drop in Nickel Prices Weighed on Market Confidence, High-Grade NPI Price Center Pulled Back
summary: A sharp drop in nickel prices on March 19 weighed heavily on market confidence, leading to a pullback in high-grade NPI prices. The review details the impact on nickel pig iron markets. This reflects broader pressures in the nickel sector.

title: Nickel Headed to $20,000 as Indonesia Limits Production, EV Demand Surges
summary: Indonesia has set 2026 nickel ore production targets at 250-260 million tons, flat-to-down from 2025 levels, supporting nickel prices toward $20,000 per ton. EV demand grows robustly at 20% annually across regions like Europe (30%), China (17%), and the rest of the world (48%). Canada Nickel advances its Crawford project targeting 2026 construction amid emerging supply deficits.

title: Indonesia Shakes Global Nickel Market: World's Largest Mine Sees 70% Production Cut in Push to Inflate Prices
summary: Indonesia slashed PT Weda Bay Nickel's 2026 production quota to 12 million wet metric tonnes, a 71% cut from 42 million in 2025, shaking global nickel markets. The move aims to curb oversupply and boost prices, potentially creating ore shortages forcing smelters to import. Analysts see this establishing a new price floor near $17,500 per tonne.

title: Nickel Futures Surge to 19-Month High
summary: Nickel futures hit $18,900 per tonne, a 19-month high, after Indonesia confirmed 2026 ore quotas at 250-260 million wet tons, down 34% from 2025's 379 million. This curbs oversupply while balancing domestic processing and downstream development amid crackdowns on illegal mining. Strong demand from China's stainless steel and EV sectors supports the rally.

title: 3-Month Nickel Commodity Price Rises Toward US$17,420/Ton Level (Tuesday, March 17, 2026)
summary: The 3-month forward nickel price rose 0.49% to US$17,420 per ton on March 17, 2026, with 2.99% year-to-date growth and a weekly average of US$17,480. This marks the highest weekly jump, amid an upward trend over the past week and 12 months, despite a five-year annual downward average.

title: Nickel Prices Hit $18,000 in 2026 Amid Global Oversupply, US Boosts Domestic Supply Chain
summary: Nickel prices reached $18,000 in 2026 amid volatility from Indonesia's potential 34% output cut and mine halts by Vale, alongside improving Chinese demand from stainless steel and EVs. The US advances domestic supply via Alaska's Nikolai project under FAST-41, aligning with critical minerals policies to reduce foreign reliance.

title: Re-energising the nickel industry through sustainable battery production
summary: Queensland Pacific Metals is developing the TECH Project to become a leading supplier of high-grade, ethically derived nickel for EV batteries using the patented DNi Process™. The project imports nickel laterite ore from New Caledonia to a carbon-negative facility in Townsville, Australia, with offtake agreements from LG Energy Solution and POSCO. This supports sustainable production of battery metals amid the green energy transition.

title: Nickel Industry News, Views and Commentary - Chronickels
summary: The Nickel Institute blog features articles on sustainability, treatment processes, and innovations in nickel production, including NOVONIX’s patented all-dry, zero-waste cathode synthesis technology to reduce costs and environmental impact of nickel-based cathodes. Other topics cover green hydrogen using nickel-containing stainless steel, nickel's role in cellulosic ethanol production, and sustainability across the nickel value chain. It addresses concerns about nickel supply for electric vehicles and cleaner energy.

title: Nickel nationalism holds back Indonesia's clean tech ambitions
summary: Indonesia's nickel nationalism is hindering its clean technology ambitions by focusing on raw processing rather than advanced capabilities. The article calls for a shift to capability-centric, technology-neutral green industrial policy to transform the nickel boom into sustainable clean tech development.
